author: anon-ab6221f8
content:
## What

Add the `agent-todo: fix` dispatch rule. Fix agents auto-dispatch with VerifyLevel::Ci — they push a branch and open a draft PR for human review (Q2 decision).

### Triage rule
Add to the match in dispatch.rs:
```rust
"agent-todo: fix" => Disposition::Dispatch {
    description: format!("Fix bug from GH#{}: {}", signal.reference, signal.title),
    scope: AgentScope {
        allowed_paths: vec!["src/".into(), "tests/".into()],
        verify: VerifyLevel::Ci,
        timeout: Duration::from_secs(3600), // 1 hour
        model,
    },
    attempt,
},
```

### Fix agent prompt
Scoped prompt for fix agents:
1. Read the issue
2. Explore the codebase to find root cause
3. Write a failing test
4. Implement the fix
5. Run full test suite
6. Record findings via crosslink comments
7. Push branch and open draft PR

PR title format: `fix: <issue title> (sentinel #<dispatch-id>)`

### VerifyLevel::Ci requirements
Unlike Local, Ci means:
- Agent is allowed to push (need to configure git push in allowed_tools)
- Agent creates a draft PR via `gh pr create --draft`
- Agent needs `gh auth` credentials in the worktree

### Credential propagation
The agent worktree needs to be able to push. Options:
- Copy the user's git credential helper config to the worktree's local .gitconfig
- Set GH_TOKEN environment variable at launch time (read from `gh auth token`)
- Best approach: at launch time, run `gh auth token` to get the token, pass it as GH_TOKEN env var to the tmux session. This avoids touching git config.

### Fix result template
New template variant for fix dispatches:
- PR link instead of test file
- Files changed count + lines diff
- Changes summary section

### Dependencies
- #654 (dispatch engine — add rule to triage)
- #655 (collection — fix template for GH comment)
- #658 (config — "agent-todo: fix" in labels list)
- All V0 issues (fix builds on the infrastructure)
